sections.paragraph:
εὐθύτης, ητος, ἡ ‘straightness’ (Aristot. et al.) in our lit. only fig. (LXX; PsSol 2:15; TestIss 3:1; TestGad 7:7; ApcMos 27; Syntipas 125, 8; Psellus p. 238, 1 with δικαιοσύνη and φιλανθρωπία) righteousness, uprightness ἡ ῥάβδος τῆς εὐθύτητος (gen. of quality) the righteous scepter Hb 1:8 (Ps 44:7); 1 Cl 14:5 (Ps 36:37). πορεύεσθαι ἐν τῇ εὐ. τοῦ κυρίου walk in the uprightness of the Lord Hv 3, 5, 3.—DELG s.v. εὐθύς.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

gen. gen. = genitive

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 406
